skill.name: deployment-audit-skill
skill.description: Use DigitalOcean, Vercel, and local project checks to audit deployment health, crash logs, environment consistency, build status, and runtime issues.
skill.summary: Audit deployment health, logs, environment settings, builds, and API readiness.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found Markdown command examples, environment file references, and secret variable names. Review confirmed no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration, but the skill intentionally handles secrets, deployment CLIs, and networked health checks. Publish with a warning about secret redaction and user-owned deployments.

medium_findings.title: Environment Secret Review Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to compare .env.local, Vercel variables, and DigitalOcean app specs. This is legitimate for deployment audits, but outputs can expose secrets if values are copied.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names secret-bearing variables and directs comparison of local and hosted environment settings. No evidence found of exfiltration or instruction to print secret values.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents doctl, npm build, and npm install commands. These are normal DevOps actions, but command execution can expose deployment data or alter dependencies.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and match the static findings. They are operational commands for a deployment audit, not evidence of malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Networked Deployment And API Checks
medium_findings.description: The skill directs use of DigitalOcean, Vercel, API endpoint checks, database connectivity checks, and external service validation. This network access is expected but should stay limited to user-owned systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require deployment and API health checks through external services. No unauthorized destination or data transfer pattern was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static blocker points to the frontmatter description. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or encryption code appears at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata about deployment health checks. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes an invalid environment variable issue. It does not request host enumeration, user enumeration, or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a troubleshooting heading for environment variables. It does not contain reconnaissance commands or discovery logic.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Several static hits are Markdown inline code or fenced lists, not executable Ruby backticks. The real command examples are tracked separately as medium risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax is Markdown formatting in SKILL.md, not Ruby code. The file contains no Ruby interpreter context.

dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks agents to compare .env.local, Vercel variables, and DigitalOcean app specs. This is legitimate for deployment audits, but outputs can expose secrets if values are copied.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names secret-bearing variables and directs comparison of local and hosted environment settings. No evidence found of exfiltration or instruction to print secret values.

dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and match the static findings. They are operational commands for a deployment audit, not evidence of malicious execution.

content.value_statement: Deployment failures are hard to diagnose when logs, builds, and environment settings drift across platforms. This skill guides a focused audit of DigitalOcean, Vercel, local build output, and API health.
actual_capabilities: Reviews DigitalOcean and Vercel deployment health signals.
Guides retrieval and review of crash logs.
Compares local, Vercel, and DigitalOcean environment settings.
Checks build output, TypeScript errors, routes, and dependencies.
Validates API health, authentication, database access, and service connections.
Records human-readable deployment audit summaries and issue entries.
limitations: Requires access to the relevant DigitalOcean, Vercel, and project tooling.
Does not provide automated remediation scripts.
Can expose secrets if users paste full environment values into prompts.
Focused on Synthex and Unite-Hub deployment patterns.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Failed Deployments
use_cases.description: Investigate crash logs, build errors, missing dependencies, and route compilation failures after a broken release.

use_cases.title: Verify Environment Consistency
use_cases.description: Compare local, Vercel, and DigitalOcean settings before promoting a deployment to production.

use_cases.title: Prepare Health Reports
use_cases.description: Create a readable deployment status summary with tracked issues for follow-up work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the current deployment health. Check recent deploy status, crash logs, build output, and obvious runtime errors. Redact all secret values.

prompt_templates.title: Environment Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare local, Vercel, and DigitalOcean environment variable names. Report missing, mismatched, or suspicious settings without printing secret values.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate build readiness, route compilation, authentication flow, database connectivity, API health, and external service configuration for this release.

prompt_templates.title: Incident Triage Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Review deployment logs, crash patterns, memory signals, slow endpoints, and recent environment changes. Produce a prioritized incident report with owners and next actions.

output_examples.input: Check why the latest deployment keeps restarting.
output: Deployment status summary with the affected component and restart window.
Likely cause list based on logs, build output, and environment checks.
Prioritized next actions with secret values redacted.

output_examples.input: Verify environment settings before production release.
output: Comparison of required variable names across local, Vercel, and DigitalOcean settings.
Missing or mismatched entries grouped by service area.
Release readiness notes and blocking issues.

output_examples.input: Create a deployment audit summary for the team.
output: Open issue register entries with severity and recommended owner.
Follow-up checks for build, runtime, API, and database health.

best_practices: Redact secret values before sharing logs, environment settings, or audit notes.
Run commands only against user-owned projects and approved deployment accounts.
Record evidence, severity, owner, and next action for every deployment issue.
anti_patterns: Do not paste full API keys, service role keys, or webhook secrets into prompts.
Do not run install or deployment commands without reviewing their impact first.
Do not treat a successful build as proof of runtime or API health.

faq.question: What platforms does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on DigitalOcean, Vercel, local builds, Supabase, Stripe, and related API health checks.

faq.question: Does it need access to deployment tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Useful results require approved access to project logs, app specs, build tools, and environment settings.

faq.question: Can it safely inspect environment variables?
faq.answer: It can compare variable names and presence. Users should redact values and avoid sharing secrets.

faq.question: Does it fix deployment issues automatically?
faq.answer: No. It identifies likely causes and recommends next actions for a human operator.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to one project?
faq.answer: It names Synthex and Unite-Hub, but the workflow can help similar web deployments.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses deployment commands, network checks, and secret-adjacent configuration review. No malicious intent was found.
